summary: Typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer ships cross-platform clipboard-hijacking cryptocurrency clipper with persistence
details: @jaggle/resizeobserves impersonates the popular @juggle/resize-observer package (the README even imports '@juggle/resize-observer'), but contains no ResizeObserver code. Its postinstall script (npm-install.cjs) pip-installs a bundled Python 'clipboard-guardian' package using --break-system-packages, then establishes OS-level persistence on every platform: a systemd user unit named 'python3-dbus-helper.service' on Linux, a LaunchAgent 'com.apple.python.runtime' on macOS, and a 'PyRuntimeBroker' Windows Scheduled Task — all configured to relaunch on login with KeepAlive/Restart=always. Service names are deliberately chosen to mimic legitimate OS components. The installed Python daemon (guardian.py) further disguises itself with setproctitle, polls the user's clipboard, and substitutes any copied cryptocurrency address with attacker-controlled wallets hardcoded for 30+ chains (ETH 0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18, BTC bc1qs2mpls4p0f7..., XMR 42zhAidVhP7..., etc.). Every cryptocurrency transfer attempted by the developer after installation is silently redirected to the attacker. Installer harm fires at install time (persistence + daemon launch) and continues across reboots.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
